# Page 1^page=1
Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition, Chapter 12: Mass-Storage Systems

# Page 2^page=2
12.2 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Chapter 12: Mass-Storage Systems  Overview of Mass Storage Structure  Disk Structure  Disk Attachment  Disk Scheduling  Disk Management  Swap-Space Management  RAID Structure  Stable-Storage Implementation  Tertiary Storage Devices

# Page 3^page=3
12.3 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Objectives  Describe the physical structure of secondary and tertiary storage devices and the resulting effects on the uses of the devices  Explain the performance characteristics of mass-storage devices  Discuss operating-system services provided for mass storage

# Page 4^page=4
12.4 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Overview of Mass Storage Structure  Magnetic tape  Was early secondary-storage medium  Relatively permanent and holds large quantities of data  Access time slow  Mainly used for backup, storage of infrequently-used data, transfer medium between systems  20-200GB typical storage 第i 块 间 隙 第i +1块

# Page 5^page=5
12.5 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Moving-head Disk Mechanism 任何时刻只有一个磁头处于活动状态：输入输出数据流以位串形式出现 物理地址形式：磁头号（盘面号）、磁道号（柱面号）、扇区号 扇区：标题（10字节）、数据（512字节）、ECC纠错信息（12-16字节）

# Page 6^page=6
12.6 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Flash Storage  Flash storage (闪存存储)  使用半导体技术，通过电荷的方式存储数据，不需要耗时的机械操作  存储单元为浮栅晶体管（控制栅、浮栅，中间为绝缘层）  利用隧穿效应将电子注入浮栅中。浮栅中有电子，表示0；反之，表示1  主要特性：  写入与擦除循环 – 已经写入的数据不能直接以覆盖形式修改，如要修改，需要先执行以块为粒度的 擦除操作  磨损与耐久度 – 存储单元在大量写入与擦除 操作后，会发生写穿（Wear-out） 问题. – 设备控制器常使用磨损均衡 （Wear leveling）技术，尽可能让 写入操作平均分摊在不同的存储 单元中 … 页 块 面 带 闪存存储的主要结构 … … …… …

# Page 7^page=7
12.7 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Solid State Drive, SSD  Solid State Drive, SSD 固态硬盘  由多块闪存（Flash Package）和相关控制器组成  主机接口负责与主机进行通信，通常是NVMe，SATA，PCIe等接口协议中一种  并行性：固态硬盘包含多个闪存存储单元，并提供多个访问通道；每个通道可独立使用， 可同时处理多个存储请求  闪存翻译层： 以页为单位进行寻址，每页有各自的物理地址。闪存翻译层负责用于维 护逻辑地址到物理地址的映射。  垃圾回收：闪存页在写入数据后，不能直接覆盖重写，需擦除，因此为了回收旧数据， 固态硬盘控制器会记录每个页是否有效。当一个块中所有页都被无效时，控制器对这个 块进行擦除。 设备 寄存器 主机 接口 微控制器 DMA控制器 内存缓存区 闪存 控制器 总线 闪存#0 闪存#1 闪存#0 闪存#1 通道0 通道1 固态硬盘控制器

# Page 8^page=8
12.8 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Moving-head Disk Mechanism 任何时刻只有一个磁头处于活动状态：输入输出数据流以位串形式出现 物理地址形式：磁头号（盘面号）、磁道号（柱面号）、扇区号 扇区：标题（10字节）、数据（512字节）、ECC纠错信息（12-16字节）

# Page 9^page=9
12.9 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Disk Structure  Disk drives are addressed as large 1-dimensional arrays of logical blocks, where the logical block is the smallest unit of transfer  The 1-dimensional array of logical blocks is mapped into the sectors of the disk sequentially  Sector 0 is the first sector of the first track on the outermost cylinder （柱面）  Mapping proceeds in order through that track, then the rest of the tracks in that cylinder, and then through the rest of the cylinders from outermost to innermost 先最外磁道内扇区，再柱面内磁道，再从外到内柱面

# Page 10^page=10
12.10 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Overview of Mass Storage Structure  Magnetic disks provide bulk of secondary storage of modern computers  Drives rotate at 60 to 200 times per second  Transfer rate is rate at which data flow between drive and computer  Positioning time (random-access time) is time to move disk arm to desired cylinder (seek time 寻道时 间) and time for desired sector to rotate under the disk head (rotational latency 旋转延迟)  Head crash results from disk head making contact with the disk surface That can not be repaired That’s bad 转速 (RPM) 常见应用场景与特点 5400 RPM 常见于笔记本硬盘、台式机辅助存储或外置硬盘，特 点是安静、低功耗、发热量小。 7200 RPM 目前主流台式机硬盘的常见转速，在性能和功耗之间 取得平衡。 10000/15000 RPM 曾用于高性能企业级硬盘，但随着SSD的普及，这类 产品已越来越少见了

# Page 11^page=11
12.11 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Overview of Mass Storage Structure  Disks can be removable  Drive attached to computer via I/O bus  Buses vary, including IDE/ATA：连接硬盘驱动器与光盘驱动器，并行接口，传输速率较低 EIDE：ATA的升级，连接硬盘驱动器与光盘驱动器，并行接口 SATA： ATA的升级，连接硬盘驱动器、光盘驱动器和其他存储设备，串行接 口 USB： 连接外部设备，包括存储设备、打印机、键盘等，串行接口 Fiber Channel：高速网络存储接口，连接存储设备到计算机网络，速率高达 Gbps SCSI：可连接多种设备，包括硬盘、打印机、光盘驱动器等，并行或串行接口 PCIe: 高速串行总线标准，连接主板与各种外部设备。  Host controller in computer uses bus to talk to disk controller built into drive or storage array

# Page 12^page=12
12.12 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Overview of Mass Storage Structure  IO Bus的作用是充当计算机系统与磁盘之间的标准化桥梁，负责物理连接、数据传输、指令 控制和供电。  它规定了： n 物理上怎么连（接口和线缆）。 n 语言上怎么说（通信协议和指令集）。 n 性能上有多快（速度、队列深度和并发能力）。 n 功能上有哪些（是否支持热插拔、冗余等）。 为什么需要“安全弹出”？ 即使在你看似没有进行文件操作时，操作系统也可能在后台与U盘进行数据交互。主要原因如下： •写入缓存 -为了提高性能，Windows等操作系统默认会启用 “写入缓存”策略。 -“安全弹出”的作用就是：通知系统立即将缓存中的所有数据强制写入U盘，并等待U盘确认 所有操作已完成，之后切断与U盘的所有逻辑连接。 •文件系统占用 -操作系统需要管理U盘上的文件系统（如FAT32、exFAT、NTFS）。即使你没有复制文件， 系统也可能在后台更新文件的元数据，例如最后访问时间戳，或者进行文件系统的整理。

# Page 13^page=13
12.13 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Disk Attachment  Computers access disk storage in 2 ways:  Host-attached storage 主机附属存储 accessed through I/O ports talking to I/O busses 适用于小系统  Network-attached storage 网络附属存储 via a remote host in a distributed system 通过分布式文件系统的远程主机进行访问

# Page 14^page=14
12.14 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Host-attached storage  Storage accessed through local I/O ports  The typical PC uses: IDE(ATA),SATA  Workstation and server: SCSI, FC (Fiber Channel)  FC is high-speed serial architecture  Can be switched fabric with 24-bit address space – the basis of storage area networks (SANs) in which many hosts attach to many storage units

# Page 15^page=15
12.15 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Host-attached storage  IDE( Integrated Drive Electronics)  Also called ATA（Advanced Technology Attachment）  SATA: Serial ATA  SCSI itself is a bus, up to 16 devices on one cable  FC is high-speed serial architecture  Can be switched fabric with 24-bit address space – the basis of storage area networks (SANs) in which many hosts attach to many storage units

# Page 16^page=16
12.16 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Network-Attached Storage  Network-attached storage (NAS，网络连接存储) is storage made available over a network rather than over a local connection (such as a bus)  NFS and CIFS are common protocols  Implemented via remote procedure calls (RPCs) between host and storage  New iSCSI（Internet小型计算机系统接口） protocol uses IP network to carry the SCSI protocol  iSCSI 是一种用于访问远程存储设备的协议，它允许将存储设备映射为本地 块设备，类似于直接连接到计算机上的硬盘驱动器。

# Page 17^page=17
12.17 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Storage Area Network  存储区域网络  Common in large storage environments (and becoming more common)  Multiple hosts attached to multiple storage arrays – flexible

# Page 18^page=18
12.18 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Disk Scheduling  The operating system is responsible for using hardware efficiently — for the disk drives, this means having a fast access time and disk bandwidth  Access time has two major components  Seek time is the time for the disk to move the heads to the cylinder containing the desired sector  Rotational latency is the additional time waiting for the disk to rotate the desired sector to the disk head  Transfer time

# Page 19^page=19
12.19 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Seek time  寻道时间Ts: 把磁头从当前位置移动到指定磁道上所经历的时 间，它与移动的磁道数有关  该时间是启动磁盘的时间s与磁头移动n条磁道所花费的时 间之和，即 Ts = m * n +s m是常数，与磁盘驱动器的速度有关 – 对一般磁盘，m=0.2； – 对高速磁盘，m<=0.1, 磁臂启动时间约为2ms  寻道时间随寻道距离的增大而增大

# Page 20^page=20
12.20 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Rotational latency  旋转延迟Tr：指定扇区移动到磁头下面所经历的时间  与磁盘转速r有关  Tr = 1/2r  如果磁盘转速为5400 r/min，则每转需时11.1ms，平 均旋转延迟时间Tr为5.55ms

# Page 21^page=21
12.21 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Transfer time  传输时间Tt  把数据从磁盘读出，或向磁盘写入数据所经历的时间 Tt = b/(rN) – b为所读/写的字节数 – r为磁盘以秒计的旋转速度 – N为一条磁道上的字节数

# Page 22^page=22
12.22 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Access time  磁盘访问时间 T = Ts + 1/2r + b/(rN)  寻道时间与旋转延迟，基本都与所读/写的数据大小无关， 而它通常占据了访问时间的大头  随着磁盘传输速率的不断提高，数据传输时间所占的比例 更低。  Minimize seek time  Seek time  seek distance

# Page 23^page=23
12.23 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Solid State Drive, SSD  Solid State Drive, SSD 固态硬盘  由多块闪存（Flash Package）和相关控制器组成  主机接口负责与主机进行通信，通常是NVMe，SATA，PCIe等接口协议中一种  垃圾回收：闪存页在写入数据后，不能直接覆盖重写，需擦除，因此为了回收旧数据， 固态硬盘控制器会记录每个页是否有效。当一个块中所有页都被无效时，控制器对这个 块进行擦除。  并行性：固态硬盘包含多个闪存存储单元，并提供多个访问通道；每个通道可独立使用， 可同时处理多个存储请求  闪存翻译层： 以页为单位进行寻址，每页有各自的物理地址。闪存翻译层负责用于维 护逻辑地址到物理地址的映射。 设备 寄存器 主机 接口 微控制器 DMA控制器 内存缓存区 闪存 控制器 总线 闪存#0 闪存#1 闪存#0 闪存#1 通道0 通道1 固态硬盘控制器 机械硬盘的寻道和闪存的擦除均是比较慢的操作。 -机械硬盘的寻道往往出现在关键路径中，对性能有关键性影响。 -闪存的擦除较少出现在关键路径中。 通过闪存翻译层，新数据可以写在已经被擦除的块中，而旧数据可以被异步擦除。 通常只有当缓存中剩余空间较少，同时缺少足量的被擦除但未被使用的块时，才会在 关键路径上发生垃圾回收。等待垃圾回收往往会造成固态硬盘的读写性能下降。

# Page 24^page=24
12.24 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Disk Scheduling (Cont)  Several algorithms exist to schedule the servicing of disk I/O requests  We illustrate them with a request queue (0-199) 98, 183, 37, 122, 14, 124, 65, 67 Head pointer 53

# Page 25^page=25
12.25 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition FCFS Illustration shows total head movement of 640 cylinders

# Page 26^page=26
12.26 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition FCFS  简单，公平 优点  效率不高，相邻两次请求可 能会造成最内到最外的柱面 寻道，使磁头反复移动，增 加了服务时间，对机械也不 利 缺点

# Page 27^page=27
12.27 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition SSTF  Shortest-Seek-Time-Fist (SSTF) 最短寻道时间优先  Selects the request with the minimum seek time from the current head position  SSTF scheduling is a form of SJF scheduling; may cause starvation of some requests

# Page 28^page=28
12.28 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition SSTF (Cont) Illustration shows total head movement of 236 cylinders

# Page 29^page=29
12.29 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition SSTF  改善了磁盘平均服务时间 优点  造成某些访问请求长期等待 得不到服务（饥饿） 缺点

# Page 30^page=30
12.30 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition SCAN  The disk arm starts at one end of the disk, and moves toward the other end, servicing requests until it gets to the other end of the disk, where the head movement is reversed and servicing continues.  SCAN algorithm Sometimes called the elevator algorithm

# Page 31^page=31
12.31 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition SCAN (Cont.) Illustration shows total head movement of 236 cylinders 折中权衡 距离、方向

# Page 32^page=32
12.32 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition C-SCAN （单向扫描）  Provides a more uniform wait time than SCAN  The head moves from one end of the disk to the other, servicing requests as it goes  When it reaches the other end, however, it immediately returns to the beginning of the disk, without servicing any requests on the return trip  Treats the cylinders as a circular list that wraps around from the last cylinder to the first one （圆形列表）

# Page 33^page=33
12.33 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition C-SCAN (Cont) 减少了新请求的 最大延迟

# Page 34^page=34
12.34 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition LOOK  Version of SCAN  Arm only goes as far as the last request in each direction, then reverses direction immediately, without first going all the way to the end of the disk.

# Page 35^page=35
12.35 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition LOOK •Queue=98,183,37,122,14,124,65,67 •head stars at 53 •total head movement of 208 cylinders. 14 37 53 6567 98 122124 183 199 ... .. . .. . 0

# Page 36^page=36
12.36 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition C-LOOK  Version of C-SCAN  Arm only goes as far as the last request in each direction, then reverses direction immediately, without first going all the way to the end of the disk

# Page 37^page=37
12.37 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition C-LOOK (Cont)

# Page 38^page=38
12.38 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Disk-Scheduling Algorithm  旋转调度算法  根据延迟时间来决定执 行次序的调度  三种情况 （1）若干等待访问者请求访问 同一磁道上的不同扇区 （2）若干等待访问者请求访问 同一柱面不同磁头上的不同编号 的扇区 （3）若干等待访问者请求访问 不同磁头上具有相同编号的扇区

# Page 39^page=39
12.39 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Disk-Scheduling Algorithm  三种情况 （1）若干等待访问者请求访问同一磁道上的不同扇区 （2）若干等待访问者请求访问同一柱面不同磁头上的不同编号的扇 区 （3）若干等待访问者请求访问不同磁头上具有相同编号的扇区  解决方案：  对于(1)(2)：总是让首先到达读写磁头位置下的扇区先进行传送操作  对于(3)：这些扇区同时到达读写磁头位置下，可任意选择一个读写磁 头进行传送操作

# Page 40^page=40
12.40 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Disk-Scheduling Algorithm  解决方案：  对于(1)(2)：总是让首先到达读写磁头位置下的扇区先进行传送操作  对于(3)：这些扇区同时到达读写磁头位置下，可任意选择一个读写磁 头进行传送操作 请求顺序 柱面号 磁头号 扇区号 （1） 5 4 1 （2） 5 1 5 （3） 5 4 5 （4） 5 2 8

# Page 41^page=41
12.41 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Selecting a Disk-Scheduling Algorithm  SSTF is common and has a natural appeal  SCAN and C-SCAN perform better for systems that place a heavy load on the disk  Performance depends on the number and types of requests  Requests for disk service can be influenced by the file- allocation method  The disk-scheduling algorithm should be written as a separate module of the operating system, allowing it to be replaced with a different algorithm if necessary  Either SSTF or LOOK is a reasonable choice for the default algorithm

# Page 42^page=42
12.42 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition 信息的优化分布  记录在磁道上的排列方式也会影响输入输出操作的时间  例子：处理程序要求顺序处理8个记录；磁盘旋转一周为 20毫秒/周；花5毫秒对记录进行处理 1 2 4 3 85 76 1 4 2 7 65 38

# Page 43^page=43
12.43 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition  例1. 当前磁头臂位于柱面号20，此时磁盘请求柱面以10， 22，20，2，40，6，38的顺序到达，且磁头正在按照由 小到大的方向移动。假设寻道时每个柱面移动需要6ms。 计算以下调度算法的寻道时间。1）FCFS， 2）SSTF， 3 ）LOOK。

# Page 44^page=44
12.44 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition  解答  （1）FCFS  调度次序：10，22，20，2，40，6，38  磁头移动道数：10+12+2+18+38+34+32=146  146*6=876ms  (2) SSTF  调度次序： 20 ， 22 ， 10 ， 6， 2 ， 38 ， 40  磁头移动道数：0+2+12+4+4+36+2=60  60*6= 360ms  (3) LOOK  调度次序： 20，22，38，40，（反向）10，6，2  磁头移动道数：0+2+16+2+30+4+4=58  58*6=348ms

# Page 45^page=45
12.45 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Disk Management  Several other aspects of disk management  Disk formatting  Boot from disk  Bad block recovery

# Page 46^page=46
12.46 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Disk formatting  Low-level formatting, or physical formatting — Dividing a disk into sectors that the disk controller can read and write  A sector typically consists of a header, a data area (usually 512B), and a trailer  Most hard disks are low-level-formatted at the factory  To use a disk to hold files, the operating system still needs to record its own data structures on the disk  Partition the disk into one or more groups of cylinders  Logical formatting or “making a file system”  To increase efficiency most file systems group blocks into clusters Disk I/O done in blocks File I/O done in clusters

# Page 47^page=47
12.47 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Boot Block  The bootstrap program is stored in ROM  It initializes all aspects of the system, and then starts the OS by reading the boot code from MBR 负责寻找哪个设备可以启动  MBR（Master Boot Recorder，主引导记录）: the first sector( 0# ) on the hard disk 负责记录哪个分区是活动分区  Boot block: the first sector in the active partition 负责存储操作系统的第二段引导程序，它的任务是加载操作系统内核的核心 文件 Booting from a Disk in Windows 2000

# Page 48^page=48
12.48 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Bad Blocks  How to handle bad blocks?  On simple disk, such as IDE disk  Bad blocks are handled manually  The MS-DOS format command performs logical formatting  Scan the disk to find bad blocks  For bad block, it writes a special value into the FAT entry to tell the allocation routines not to use the block.  On high-end disk, such as SCSI disk 核心机制：重映射  Methods such as sector sparing（扇区备用） used to handle bad blocks  The controller maintains a list of bad blocks on the disk  The list is initialized during the low-level formatting at the factory and is updated over the life of the disk.  Low-level formatting sets aside spare sectors not visible to OS  The controller can be told to replace a bad sector logically with one of the spare sectors.

# Page 49^page=49
12.49 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Swap-Space Management  Swap-space — Virtual memory uses disk space as an extension of main memory  Swap space is used in various ways by different OSs:  Some OS use swap space to hold an entire process image including code and data segments  Paging system uses swap space to hold the paged out pages  Therefore, the amount of swap space needed on a system can vary depending on :  the amount of physical memory  the amount of virtual memory it is backing  The way (page or process?) in which the virtual memory is used  Solaris: Suggests setting swap space equal to the amount by which virtual memory exceeds pageable physical memory  Linux: Suggests setting swap space to double the amount of physical memory 物理内存的2倍

# Page 50^page=50
12.50 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Swap space Location  A swap space can reside in one of the two places:  be carved out of the normal file system  Swap space is a file （交换文件）in file system, and is managed by file system: create, name and allocation  Flexible – 交换文件相对来说更灵活，可根据需要创建、调整大小或删除。但目录的分配维护等低效。 – 交换文件可能与其他文件竞争磁盘I/O资源，这可能导致性能下降。  more commonly, in a separate disk partition  Raw partition（原始分区）, no file system and directory  Managed by swap space storage manager : allocation and deallocation  More efficient – 在生磁盘（没有格式化的磁盘，没有目录）：存取效率高。在系统初始化时就分配一个固 定大小的交换分区。

# Page 51^page=51
12.51 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition When to Allocate Swap-Space  4.3BSD allocates swap space when process starts  holds text segment (the program) and data segment  Solaris 2 allocates swap space only when a page is forced out of physical memory, not when the virtual memory page is first created

# Page 52^page=52
12.52 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Data Structures for Swapping on Linux Systems  Linux allows the use of multiple swap spaces  These swap spaces are usually put on separate disks for load balance  Kernel uses swap maps to track swap-space use

# Page 53^page=53
12.53 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition RAID Structure  RAID（Redundant Arrays of Independent Disk, 磁盘冗余阵列 ） are used for higher reliability and higher data-transfer rate.  美国加州伯克利分校D.A.Patterson教授，1988年提出  将多块磁盘按照一定要求构成一个独立的存储设备  考虑：磁盘存储系统的速度、容量、容错、数据灾难发生后的数据恢复  （1）Provides higher reliability via redundancy 通过冗余提高可靠 性  mirroring  Increases the mean time to failure  （2）Improves performance via parallelism 通过并发提高性能  Implements parallel access by data-striping 数据分条  Disk striping uses a group of disks as one storage unit  Bit-level striping  Block-level striping  RAID is arranged into six different levels

# Page 54^page=54
12.54 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition RAID Levels strip 0 strip1 strip 2 strip3 条带化，无冗余（即差错控制） 性能最佳 镜像，数据安全性 最好，磁盘利用率 50% 交错块奇偶校验 以数据块为单位 位/字节级交叉奇偶校验 只能检测错误，但不能自行纠正，用于在 整盘故障时重建数据 内存方式校验，汉明码 位级拆分+多个专用校验盘， 可自动纠错

# Page 55^page=55
12.55 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition RAID (Cont)  RAID schemes improve performance and improve the reliability of the storage system by storing redunant data  Mirroring or shadowing (RAID 1) keeps duplicate of each disk  Striped mirrors (RAID 1+0) or mirrored stripes (RAID 0+1) provides high performance and high reliability  Block interleaved parity (RAID 4, 5, 6) uses much less redundancy

# Page 56^page=56
12.56 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition RAID (0 + 1) and (1 + 0) Striped mirrors (RAID 1+0) mirrored stripes (RAID 0+1)

# Page 57^page=57
12.57 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition RAID (0 + 1) and (1 + 0) RAID 1+0 A RAID 1+0 array is implemented as RAID 0, whose elements are RAID 1 RAID 0+1 RAID 0+1 means arrays implemented as RAID 1, whose elements are RAID 0 arrays. R A ID 0 R A ID 1 mirrored stripes Striped mirrorsR A ID 1 R A ID 0 D is k 0 D is k 1 D is k 2 D is k 3 D is k 0 D is k 1 D is k 3D is k 2 如果一个磁盘故障，整个条不能访问。 如果一个磁盘故障，但它的镜像仍然 如其他磁盘一样可用。 a) Disk0和2不能同时故障，此时两个RAID0都故障，数据不能读取； b) Disk0和3不能同时故障，此时两个RAID0都故障，数据不能读取； c) Disk1和2不能同时故障，此时两个RAID0都故障，数据不能读取； d) Disk1和3不能同时故障，此时两个RAID0都故障，数据不能读取； a) Disk0和Disk1不能同时损坏，否则数据不能正常读取； b) Disk2和Disk3不能同时损坏，否则数据也不能正常读取； 更鲁棒

# Page 58^page=58
12.58 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition ZFS Checksums All Metadata and Data  RAID alone does not prevent or detect data corruption or other errors, just disk failures  ZFS （ Zettabyte File System ）代表了存储管理技术的进化 ，它不仅关注数据的性能优化，还提供了数据完整性保护， 是现代操作系统和大规模存储环境中的重要技术选择  ZFS内部包含类似RAID的功能，称为RAID-Z  是一种软件层解决方案：将文件系统和RAID的功能整合在一起  RAID作为硬件或软件层的解决方案，位于文件系统和物理磁盘之间 ，为文件系统提供更高的性能和可靠性 ZFS 的首席设计师Jeff Bonwick曾在其博客中说明， 他们决定将这个新文件系统命名为“ZFS”，仅仅 是因为： •“Z” 是最后一个可用的字母（在他们当时已使用 的文件系统代号中）。 •它暗示这是最后一个（The Last） 需要的文件系统， 因为其设计极具前瞻性和可扩展性。 单位名称 缩写 换算关系（以字 节计） 形象化的理解与举例 Byte B 1 Byte 一个英文字符。 Kilobyte KB 10³ (1,000) Bytes 一段简短的纯文本。 Megabyte 兆 MB 10⁶ (1,000,000) Bytes 一张早期的手机壁纸，或 一首MP3歌曲的几分钟。 Gigabyte 级 GB 10⁹ (1,000,000,000) Bytes 一部高清电影，或一部智 能手机的存储容量（早 期）。 Terabyte 太 TB 10¹² (1,000,000,000, 000) Bytes 约2块现代台式机硬盘的 容量，或一家大型书店的 所有书目信息。 Petabyte 拍 PB 10¹⁵ Bytes 美国国家航空航天局 （NASA）每天从地球观 测卫星接收的数据量可达 PB级。 Exabyte 艾 EB 10¹⁸ Bytes 全球互联网在特定高峰时 刻的月度流量可达EB级。 Zettabyte 泽 ZB 10²¹ Bytes 到2025年，预计全球产生 的年度数据总量将达到ZB 级（数百ZB）。 Yottabyte 尧 YB 10²⁴ Bytes 目前这是一个理论级单位。 用于描述：全人类在数字 时代产生的所有数据总和， 或未来全球数据量的远景。

# Page 59^page=59
12.59 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition ZFS Checksums All Metadata and Data  Solaris ZFS adds checksums of all data and metadata  Checksums kept with pointer to object, to detect if object is the right one and whether it changed  Can detect and correct data and metadata corruption

# Page 60^page=60
12.60 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition ZFS Checksums All Metadata and Data  ZFS also removes volumes, partititions  Disks allocated in pools  File systems with a pool share that pool, use and release space like “malloc” and “free” memory allocate / release calls  ZFS 的存储池（Storage Pool）可以动态调整，磁盘可以 在线添加或移除，而 传统RAID添加或移除磁盘通常需要 重新配置整个阵列。

# Page 61^page=61
12.61 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Traditional and Pooled Storage

# Page 62^page=62
12.62 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Stable-Storage Implementation  stable storage definition  Information is never lost  To implement stable storage:  Replicate information on more than one nonvolatile storage media with independent failure modes  Update information in a controlled manner to ensure that we can recover the stable data after any failure during data transfer or recovery

# Page 63^page=63
12.63 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Tertiary Storage Devices  Low cost is the defining characteristic of tertiary storage  Generally, tertiary storage is built using removable media  floppy disks  CD-ROMs  DVDs  WORM disks (“Write Once, Read Many Times”) e.g., CD, DVD  Tapes
